chapter_title.ar: باب وَصِيَّةِ الْغُلَامِ وَ الْجَارِيَةِ الَّتِي لَمْ تُدْرِكْ وَ مَا يَجُوزُ مِنْهَا وَ مَا لَا يَجُوزُ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 21 – Bequest of the boy and the maid who has not attained awareness (puberty), and what is allowed from it, and is not allowed

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (23/47)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (23/47)
text: إِذَا بَلَغَ الْغُلَامُ عَشْرَ سِنِينَ فَأَوْصَى بِثُلُثِ مَالِهِ فِي حَقٍّ جَازَتْ وَصِيَّتُهُ فَإِذَا كَانَ ابْنَ سَبْعِ سِنِينَ فَأَوْصَى مِنْ مَالِهِ بِالْيَسِيرِ فِي حَقٍّ جَازَتْ وَصِيَّتُهُ .

en.hubeali: Hameed Bin Ziyad, from Al Hassan Bin Muhammad Bin Sama’at, from Abdullah Bin Jabala, from Abu Al Magra, from Abu Baseer,

Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> has said: ‘When the boy reaches ten years, so he bequeaths with a third of his wealth rightfully, his bequest is allowed. So if he was a boy of seven years, and he bequeaths with a little in a rightful way, his bequest is allowed’.
en.sarwar: 4. Humayd ibn Ziyad has narrated from al-Hassan ibn Muhammad ibn Sama‘ah from ‘Abd Allah ibn Jabalah from abu al-Mighra’ from abu Basir who has narrated the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘When a boy becomes ten years old and makes a will about one-third of his assets for a right cause, his will is permissible; but when he is seven years old his will about only a small amount of assets is permissible.’”
